title: dryopteris leucostipes
intro: Dryopteris leucostipes, also known as the white-stiped wood fern, is a species of fern native to China. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, helping to soothe and heal wounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of bronchitis, as well as acting as an antimicrobial agent to prevent infections. In herbal preparations, the roots of the Dryopteris leucostipes plant can be made into tea or powder, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments.
